rules: buffer identity beats sliced precompiled-call inputs (callify materializes slices, reads buffers directly) -- principle #12
S48/base/TK16/VEC1/U1 is the decode oracle for 8B gfx1100 (policy + generated-tile searched & closed)
S96 split overhead: more KV-splits add combine cost (-1.1..-1.5% W==D)
generated tile constants (TK/VEC/UNROLL) change only local schedule within the same primitive boundary -> do not transfer
whole-cache strided-read slope is steeper than a contiguous slice (1.43x vs llama) but the residual is <2% long-ctx-only -> low priority (crossover beyond MAXC)
decode T=1 must manufacture parallel work from KV-splits + GQA columns (owned tile does; fusion that cuts workgroups is harmful)
